events.title: German Workers' Party Founded
events.description: Anton Drexler founds the Deutsche Arbeiterpartei (DAP) in Munich. Adolf Hitler attends his first meeting on 12 September 1919 as an army spy and joins as member No. 555.

events.title: Party Renamed and 25-Point Programme
events.description: The DAP is renamed the Nationalsozialistische Deutsche Arbeiterpartei (NSDAP). Hitler presents the 25-Point Programme calling for German expansion, racial purity, and rejection of the Versailles Treaty.

events.title: Hitler Becomes Party Leader
events.description: Hitler assumes leadership of the NSDAP with near-dictatorial powers. He restructures the party and establishes the Sturmabteilung (SA) as the party's paramilitary wing.

events.title: Beer Hall Putsch
events.description: Hitler and General Ludendorff attempt to seize power in Munich, modelled on Mussolini's March on Rome. The putsch fails; Hitler is arrested and sentenced to five years (serves nine months). He dictates 'Mein Kampf' during his imprisonment.

events.title: NSDAP Refounded
events.description: After release from prison, Hitler refounds the NSDAP, this time committing to the 'legal path' to power. He establishes the SS as his personal bodyguard.

events.title: Wall Street Crash — Rise of Extremism
events.description: The Great Depression devastates Germany. Unemployment rises to 6 million by 1932. The NSDAP's share of the vote rises from 2.6% (1928) to 18.3% (September 1930) and 37.4% (July 1932).

events.title: Presidential Election
events.description: Hitler challenges Hindenburg for the Presidency, winning 30.1% in the first round and 36.8% in the runoff. Hindenburg retains the presidency with 53% of the vote.

events.title: Hitler Appointed Chancellor
events.description: President Hindenburg appoints Hitler as Chancellor of Germany, believing conservative advisors could control him. Within months the Nazis would dismantle the Weimar Republic.

events.title: Reichstag Fire
events.description: The Reichstag parliament building is set ablaze. The Nazis blame the Communists; the Reichstag Fire Decree suspends civil liberties and enables mass arrests of political opponents.

events.title: Enabling Act
events.description: The Reichstag passes the Enabling Act (Ermächtigungsgesetz), allowing Hitler's cabinet to enact laws without Reichstag approval. The Weimar Republic effectively ends; Germany becomes a dictatorship.

events.title: Night of the Long Knives
events.description: Hitler orders the murder of SA leadership (Ernst Röhm and others) who had grown too powerful. At least 85 people are killed; the SS and Wehrmacht emerge strengthened.

events.title: Nuremberg Laws
events.description: The Reich Citizenship Law and the Law for the Protection of German Blood and Honour strip Jews of citizenship and prohibit marriage between Jews and non-Jews.

events.title: Remilitarisation of the Rhineland
events.description: German troops march into the demilitarised Rhineland zone in violation of the Treaty of Versailles. France and Britain protest but take no action, emboldening Hitler.

events.title: Anschluss — Annexation of Austria
events.description: German troops march into Austria; Austria is annexed (Anschluss). A plebiscite shows 99.73% approval. The annexation violates the Treaty of Versailles but goes unopposed.

events.title: Kristallnacht
events.description: 'Night of Broken Glass': coordinated nationwide pogrom against Jews. Over 7,500 Jewish businesses destroyed, 1,400 synagogues burned, 91 Jews killed, and 30,000 arrested and sent to concentration camps.

events.title: Germany Invades Poland — World War II Begins
events.description: Germany invades Poland using the false pretext of Polish border provocations (the Gleiwitz incident). Britain and France declare war on Germany on 3 September. World War II begins.

events.title: Operation Barbarossa
events.description: Germany invades the Soviet Union with 3 million troops on a 2,900 km front. The operation represents the full implementation of Nazi racial ideology — a war of extermination against 'Jewish Bolshevism'.

events.title: Wannsee Conference
events.description: Senior Nazi officials meet in Berlin to coordinate the 'Final Solution to the Jewish Question' — the systematic murder of all European Jews. The Holocaust enters its industrial phase.

events.title: Stalingrad Surrender
events.description: German 6th Army surrenders at Stalingrad. The defeat marks the psychological turning point of the war. German strategic offensive capability on the Eastern Front is permanently broken.

events.title: July Plot — Assassination Attempt on Hitler
events.description: Colonel Claus von Stauffenberg places a bomb at Hitler's headquarters. Hitler survives; the conspirators are arrested and executed. The failed plot triggers brutal reprisals against the military and civilian resistance.

events.title: Hitler's Suicide
events.description: With Soviet forces fighting in Berlin, Hitler marries Eva Braun, dictates his political testament blaming the Jews for the war, and commits suicide in the Führerbunker. Germany surrenders unconditionally on 8 May 1945.

events.title: NSDAP Formally Banned
events.description: The Allied Control Council formally bans the NSDAP and all its affiliated organizations. Nazi symbols, uniforms, and propaganda are prohibited. The Nuremberg Trials begin on 20 November 1945.
